section.heading: Employee Expense Report
section.text: An Expense Report (28-1448-5060) must be submitted for:

ul.li: reimbursement of personal vehicle mileage expenses for City-related travel.

section.text: A separate Expense Report must normally be submitted for each instance of travel. However, more than one (1) such trip may be listed on a single Expense Report if reimbursement is not required for lodging.

An Expense Report must also be used to request reimbursement for other legitimate expenses. The following non-exclusive examples are items which may be reimbursable expenses when incurred for City business: telephone calls, wireless phone calls which are circled on original bill, parking meter fees, parking lots with receipts, and subway and light rail transportation.

para.name: deadline-for-submission-of-report
para.heading: DEADLINE FOR SUBMISSION OF REPORT
para.text: The Expense Report must be received by the Bureau of Accounting and Payroll Services within 30 days of the last calendar day of the month in which the expenses were incurred. Expenses submitted after this time limit will not be reimbursed without written approval of the Board of Estimates.
The Expense Report must be received by the Bureau of Accounting and Payroll Services within 30 days from the day on which the employee returns from the trip.

para.name: maryland-sales-tax
para.heading: MARYLAND SALES TAX
para.text: The City is exempt from payment of the Maryland sales tax. The City's tax exemption, 3000055-9, must be supplied to the vendor or supplier at the time of purchase. Reimbursement of the Maryland sales tax will not be made in those cases where an agency or employee fails to use the City's tax exemption number.

para.name: required-receipts
para.heading: REQUIRED RECEIPTS
para.text: An Expense Report must be accompanied by the appropriate receipts, as set forth above.
